text:
BOTTOM LINE — Broadly flat tape, semis led but small caps faded. Semiconductors saved the day with SOXX +0.45%, but macro hedge QQQ was flat and SPY -0.16%, capping a weak session where oil’s +1.25% gains failed to energize cyclicals while breadth contracted, signaling a rotation into defensives and a low-conviction bid.

INDICES — VIX 18.6 +1.1pt. SOXX +0.45%, QQQ +0.10%, SPY -0.16%, DIA -0.55%, IWM -0.59%. Large > small by 0.69pp. Oil +1.25% was the standout cross-asset gainer; TLT -0.75% dragged bonds.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.1x (elevated) with price down = mild distribution; QQQ vol_ratio 0.7x (low) with price flat = ignored. XLF 1.3x volume on a -0.39% day signals financial distribution, while XLV 0.6x volume shows healthcare sector fading, not attracting fresh shorts.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE +0.45%, XLC +0.14%, XLK +0.07%, XLF -0.39%, XLP -0.39%, XLRE -0.42%, XLU -0.51%, XLI -0.72%, XLY -0.72%, XLB -0.99%, XLV -1.14%. Top industries: WGMI +10.78%, FXI +2.67%. Bottom: TAN -2.24%, XBI -2.15%. Money rotating out of materials and healthcare into energy and staples — a clear defensive tilt with no conviction on cyclicals. Rotation state: defensive risk-off.

POSITIONING — QQQ PCR 2.14 signals extreme hedging demand vs complacent SPY PCR 1.06. VIX at 18.6 is above its 20-day MA of 17.1, indicating elevated fear on rate sensitivities. No GEX flip data, but skew on QQQ at +16.2% confirms heavy put premium in tech.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +1.25% failed to lift energy sector XLE +0.45%, a divergence signaling either supply-driven moves or a negative demand read. Gold -0.22% flat despite equity weakness highlights no safe-haven bid.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.9 Greed. Top mover: WGMI +10.78% (crypto mining). Momentum extremes: best sector XLE +5.98% 20d, worst XLB -3.83% 20d. DHI -3.08% on 2.2x vol spike shows housing distribution. Watch: FXI +2.67% on elevated volume breakout; TAN -2.24% as solar distribution persists; XLV -1.14% on declining volume suggests a potential bottom if volume returns.
